1.description: Time series of zero-coupon Treasury yields This figure illustrates the decline in long-term risk-free interest rates over our sample period (January 1986 to December 2020). The plot contains the time series of zero-coupon Treasury yields at maturities of 1 year, 10 years, and 30 years. Zero-coupon yields are from the updated term structure data provided by the Federal Reserve following the approach in Gürkaynak, Sack, and Wright (2007).

2.description: Cumulative log returns of corporate bond portfolios This figure presents the cumulative log returns of corporate bond portfolios sorted by credit rating. Bonds are sorted into investment-grade (BBB- and higher) and high-yield (BB+ and lower) value-weighted portfolios. Corporate Bond is the realized portfolio return. Treasury is the duration-matched Treasury return defined in Section 1.1. Duration-Adjusted is the difference between the corporate bond return and the duration-matched Treasury return.
